channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Le projet de loi d’Ottawa sur la sécurité numérique sera présenté ce mercredi
item.description: Le gouvernement fédéral va présenter mercredi un projet de loi portant sur la sécurité des réseaux sociaux et des agents conversationnels basés sur l’intelligence artificielle (IA).

item.title: Fin de SFR : comment Free, Orange et Bouygues vont se partager vos forfaits
item.description: C'est le plus gros séisme des télécoms français depuis 2012. SFR tire sa révérence, racheté pour plus de 20 milliards d'euros par ses trois rivaux historiques qui se partagent les abonnés.

item.title: This Microsoft Defender zero-day could give hackers unprecedented access to your system
item.description: Chaotic Eclipse strikes again, releasing their seventh zero-day in just two months.

item.title: Votre vieux lave-vaisselle ou votre smartphone bientôt réparable avec une imprimante 3D ? La France prépare un décret pour 2027
item.description: Un projet de décret veut forcer les fabricants à livrer les plans de leurs pièces introuvables pour qu’on les imprime en 3D. Sur le papier, votre vieux lave-vaisselle a une seconde vie : en pratique, c’est plus compliqué.

item.title: Amazon's 'Story So Far' feature is finally rolling out to Kindles
item.description: Amazon's "Story So Far" feature is hitting Kindle hardware and the iOS app, but sadly not the Android version just yet.

item.title: Buying a new classic 6-inch Kindle? These are the best cases to bundle in with your purchase
item.description: The basic Kindle is smaller than the others, you can't pick up just any case — these are the ones that'll definitely fit.

item.title: Take a deep look at Halo: Campaign Evolved before it launches next month
item.description: This remake of the first Halo game's hits consoles on July 28.

item.title: You can personalize your Instagram algorithm now — unless you want to see more posts from accounts you follow
item.description: Instagram has expanded its algorithm personalization features to its main feed.

item.title: Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions
item.description: Anthropic just released Claude Fable 5, calling it the most powerful AI model it has ever made widely available and praising its skills in biology, among others. But the model won't answer basic biology questions - the kind you'd expect a high schooler to handle. Instead, it hands off the query to the former flagship […]

item.title: Ubisoft reportedly shuts down more studios and lays off staff in Barcelona and San Francisco
item.description: Ubisoft is reportedly trying to cut costs without leaving key franchises like Rainbow Six: Siege in the lurch with a new round of layoffs.

item.title: OpenClaw AI agent tricked into phishing attacks, with user data compromised
item.description: Varonis has a suggestion on how to make AI agents more careful.

item.title: The Best USB-C Cables (2026): for Smartphones, Tablets, and Laptops
item.description: Unravel the tangled world of cords and find the ones you need to charge your gadgets and transfer data.

item.title: Cracker Barrel stock just hit a 2026 high. Is the infamous logo discourse finally in the past?
item.description: The Southern-themed restaurant chain reported a decrease in revenue for its fiscal third quarter, but it far exceeded Wall Street’s expectations. After a controversy-filled year, Cracker Barrel Old Country Store is getting a significant stock market boost on Wednesday following a positive earnings report for its 2026 fiscal third quarter.

item.title: Quand l’US Navy a traqué un objet en forme de « Tic Tac » : l’ovni qui a changé le débat
item.description: En novembre 2004, au large de la Californie, des pilotes de chasse américains et des opérateurs radar du porte-avions USS Nimitz et du croiseur USS Princeton rapportent l’observation d’un objet blanc, lisse, sans ailes ni réacteur visible, capable de manœuvres déroutantes et d'accélérations...

item.title: Pennsylvanian lawmakers want new smart glasses safety rules — and for once a government is making a sensible technology decision
item.description: Pennsylvanian lawmakers want to make private recording with smart glasses a crime.

item.title: The luxury housing boom is unraveling. These are the only markets still getting more expensive
item.description: Most pandemic-era luxury gains have disappeared. A new report shows which cities are still defying gravity. The pandemic-era home price explosion was especially pronounced for luxury homes, but more than five years later, most of that growth has fizzled out.

item.title: Is Gemini down? Google acknowledges something went wrong errors.
item.description: Google Gemini is down across web, iOS, Android, and Chrome for many U.S. users. Google says a fix is in progress but didn't provide an ETA.

item.title: Microsoft, like, totally gets why students are booing AI-pilled graduation speakers
item.description: New college graduates around the country have been booing and heckling commencement speakers who hype up AI. Microsoft would like everyone to talk it out. In a blog post running more than 3,100 words, Microsoft vice chair and president Brad Smith addressed the recent spate of viral clips from graduation ceremonies, like former Google CEO […]

item.title: The future of AI regulation is courting the strangest, most anxious bedfellows
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about tech politics, tech influence, and tech shenanigans in Washington, DC. (If you're not a subscriber, you can get on board here.) We're back after a two-week hiatus, during most of which I was gallivanting in the Netherlands for a family wedding, and a trip […]
